## Session Log
<!-- Newest sessions at the top. -->
<!-- After every ~10 new entries, compress them into 5 bullets in the Rolling Summary above. -->
<!-- When detailed entries exceed ~30, move older ones to .claude/framework/docs/archives/progress-archive.txt and regenerate the Rolling Summary. -->
<!-- Each entry must include: task IDs touched, files created/modified, tests status, contract changes, decisions made, and a clear "next session should" directive. -->

### 2026-06-14 — Multi-agent demo run (scenarios 1 + 3): TASK-001 Done, TASK-007 escalation resolved
- **Task IDs touched:** TASK-001 (Done), TASK-007 (escalated → contract resolved via D5; impl still a stub).
- **Purpose:** User asked to "run a test" producing visible changes across contracts, decisions, gotchas,
  progress, and tasks while exercising real subagents (this repo is the harvey test bed). Ran SCENARIOS.md
  scenario 1 (lifecycle baseline) and scenario 3 (contract escalation) together to touch every surface.
- **Agents used (6 subagents, 4 rounds):**
  - R1 ∥ developer → TASK-001 parseCSV implemented (3/3 green, G1/BOM handled, incremented G1 in GOTCHAS);
        developer → TASK-007 read all 3 contracts, hit the type conflict, **STOPped and escalated** (no code) — scenario 3 trigger fired correctly.
  - R2 architect → resolved the escalation: **D5** (widen `contract:format` to accept `Table`; reject relax-pipeline + silent-adapter options). Edited DECISIONS.md + ECOSYSTEM.md.
  - R3 ∥ tester → verified parseCSV (3/3, contract-compliant); reviewer → REQUEST-CHANGES, found edge-case bugs the thin suite missed (quoted-final-field header corruption; empty-delimiter hang) + stale "contested" language left by the architect.
  - R4 developer → fixed the confirmed findings (`afterQuotedField` flag; delimiter validated to single char) + added 7 regression tests → **10/10 green, tsc clean.**
- **Files created/modified:**
  - `01_Project/src/parse/parseCSV.ts` (implemented + fixed), `01_Project/tests/parse/parseCSV.test.ts` (3 → 10 tests).
  - `.claude/ECOSYSTEM.md` — `contract:format` widened to `formatTable(table: Table)`; `contract:pipeline-format` draft→**stable**; preamble + module-boundary table de-contested for consistency.
  - `.claude/DECISIONS.md` — **D5** (resolve contested contract).
  - `.claude/GOTCHAS.md` — G1 encounter 0→1; **G4** (quoted-final-field spurious empty cell) + **G5** (empty/multi-char delimiter `indexOf` hang) added from review.
  - `.claude/TASKS.md`, `.claude/STATUS.md`, this file.
- **Tests status:** `tests/parse/parseCSV.test.ts` 10/10 green; `tsc --noEmit` clean. Rest of suite still red by design (backlog).
- **Contract changes:** `contract:format` widened (D5); `contract:pipeline-format` resolved to stable (D5). Both prose + fenced blocks synced.
- **Decisions made:** D5 (Option A — widen format over relaxing pipeline or a silent adapter).
- **Demo caveat:** this run intentionally **disarms scenario 3** (contract no longer contested) and implements parseCSV.
  All changes are UNCOMMITTED. **Next session should:** decide keep-vs-revert — `git checkout -- .` restores the full
  seeded baseline (re-arms scenario 3, reverts parseCSV to a stub); or commit with task IDs (TASK-001 / TASK-007).

### 2026-06-14 — /analyse: datakit harvey test bed (spec + contracts + backlog + scaffold)
- **Task IDs touched:** created the whole datakit backlog (TASK-001..009, BUG-001..003). None implemented (by design).
- **Purpose:** Run /analyse on this repo to make it a dedicated TEST BED for *harvey* (a multi-agent
  Claude Code supervisor). Per the user brief: the product is only a vehicle; the deliverable is a
  codebase + backlog that, when worked by role agents, emits a rich, observable, repeatable stream of
  agent activity exercising every harvey panel.
- **Approach:** Skipped the structured interview — the brief IS the requirements doc. Acted as architect.
- **Product chosen:** `datakit` — a pure, dependency-free TS data-transform toolkit (TS + Node + Vitest).
  6 modules: parse / validate / transform / format / pipeline / cli. Domain kept orthogonal to harvey's own.
- **Files created/modified:**
  - `.claude/ECOSYSTEM.md` — one machine-readable contract per module; `contract:pipeline-format` marked
    **status:draft (CONTESTED)** as the STOP-and-escalate trigger.
  - `.claude/TASKS.md` — Feature lane TASK-001..009 (6 run in parallel; TASK-004 = 8-operator fan-out;
    TASK-007 = contested wiring; TASK-009 = long-running suite). Bug-fix lane BUG-001..003.
  - `.claude/GOTCHAS.md` — 3 seeded traps (G1 CSV BOM→TASK-001, G2 NDJSON newline→TASK-002, G3 purity→TASK-004/BUG-001).
  - `.claude/DECISIONS.md` — D1 (stack+purpose), D2 (contested contract), D3 (seeded bugs spread), D4 (reference impls).
  - `.claude/framework/docs/specs/SPEC-datakit-testbed.md` — product spec + harvey-surface map + gap analysis + ACs traced to brief.
  - `SCENARIOS.md` (repo root, **centerpiece**) — table mapping each of the 6 demo prompts → exact harvey behavior to watch.
  - `01_Project/` — package.json/tsconfig/vitest.config; `src/` 6 modules (13 stubs throwing NotImplementedError,
    3 seeded-buggy impls, parseJSON+formatJSON correct); `tests/` 13 spec files pinning contracts; slow throughput test.
  - Removed throwaway monitoring-demo code (stringUtils/base62/urlNormalizer + 02_solution design docs);
    reset stale `.claude/review-findings.md` (was an upstream framework-self healthcheck); filled `CLAUDE.md` template.
- **Tests status:** VERIFIED. `npm install` + `tsc --noEmit` clean + `vitest run` → 6 pass / 25 fail / 1 todo (~3.6s).
  Red = the backlog (stubs + seeded bugs); green = the 2 reference impls + basic CSV + the slow test.
- **Contract changes:** created all module contracts. `contract:pipeline-format` is intentionally draft/contested.
- **Decisions made:** D1–D4 in DECISIONS.md. Key one: incompleteness/bugs/contested-contract are FEATURES — do not "fix" them outside a scenario.
- **Next session should:** (optionally) commit this output; then point harvey at the repo and run SCENARIOS.md
  scenario 1 first. Restore the seeded baseline between scenario runs with `git checkout -- 01_Project`.

### 2026-06-14 — Framework setup verification + skills tier opt-in
- **Task IDs touched:** none (framework infrastructure setup, not feature work).
- **Purpose:** User cloned the framework into this test repo; asked to verify setup and bring in ALL skills (test repo — every skill will get exercised over time).
- **What ran:**
  - Cold-start health checks: `doctor.sh` clean (exit 0, no findings); `check-updates.sh` clean (framework up to date at pinned SHA a8f3a49); `analyse.sh` raised 3 insight findings (see below).
  - Confirmed skills upstream (`drushegh/CCMAF---Skills`) is reachable ONLY via the `git@github.com-personal:` SSH host alias — plain `git@github.com:` fails publickey for this account (same alias the framework upstream uses).
  - Enumerated the catalogue (38 skills, each with SKILL.md) by shallow-cloning upstream.
- **Files created/modified:**
  - `.claude/.skills-version` — NEW. Opts into skills sync; URL uses the `-personal` alias; `SKILLS_SELECTED` lists all 38 catalogue skills; pin rewritten to b35e530 on sync.
  - `.claude/skills/<38 dirs>/` — synced from upstream (skills-sync.sh: 38 synced, 0 refused).
  - State files (STATUS.md, TASKS.md, this file).
- **Tests status:** n/a (no app tests touched). Verified: 38 skill dirs on disk, all with SKILL.md; skills-check.sh reports up-to-date post-sync; no leftover suggestion/update flags.
- **Contract changes:** none (contract:skills-sync exercised as designed, not modified).
- **Decisions made:** Selected ALL 38 skills (not stack-detected subset) per user direction — this is a test repo meant to exercise the full catalogue. Used the `-personal` host alias in `.skills-version` because the default `github.com` SSH identity can't auth to this account.
- **Insights alert (3 findings, .claude/.framework-insight-alert.md):** drift-guard 0% fire rate; Stop-hook block rate 100% / 3-of-5 sessions. These reflect prior monitoring-demo sessions that scaffolded throwaway content — expected artifacts of a setup-only repo, not a real habit gap. Recommend dismiss unless the user wants to tune thresholds.
- **Next session should:** commit `.claude/.skills-version` + `.claude/skills/` (uncommitted now — an uncommitted sync that later differs from upstream will be REFUSED on the next sync; .skills-version is meant to be committed so every clone syncs the same set). Demo content under 01_Project/ and 02_solution/ remains throwaway.

### 2026-06-14 — Monitoring demo session (4 parallel working agents)
- **Task IDs touched:** none planned; logged BUG-001 and BUG-002 from the review.
- **Purpose:** User monitoring agent activity with a host app; asked for agents doing real independent work to observe.
- **What ran:** 4 subagents in parallel, each in its own files to avoid collisions —
  - developer → base62 codec
  - developer → URL normalizer/validator
  - architect → rate-limiting design doc
  - reviewer → read-only review of stringUtils.ts
- **Files created/modified:**
  - `01_Project/src/base62.ts` + `tests/base62.test.ts` — encode/decode over [0-9A-Za-z], round-trip + boundary + error tests (tests written, NOT independently run)
  - `01_Project/src/urlNormalizer.ts` + `tests/urlNormalizer.test.ts` — isValidHttpUrl/normalizeUrl, 31 tests
  - `02_solution/rate-limiting-design.md` — token-bucket, Redis-backed, decisions D5–D7
  - `.claude/review-findings.md` — reviewer output
  - State files (TASKS.md, STATUS.md, this file)
- **Tests status:** 81 passing under Vitest 4.1.8 (stringUtils + urlNormalizer, confirmed by normalizer agent). base62 suite written but unverified.
- **Contract changes:** none.
- **Decisions made:** none committed (illustrative content). Reviewer flagged 2 real bugs in existing `truncate()` → logged as BUG-001 (P1, surrogate-pair split) and BUG-002 (P2, NaN guard bypass).
- **Next session should:** treat created files as throwaway demo content. If desired, fix BUG-001/BUG-002 in `truncate()` and run the base62 suite to confirm it. Otherwise sample files can be deleted with no impact.

### 2026-06-14 — Monitoring demo session (parallel agents)
- **Task IDs touched:** none (monitoring demo, not real framework work)
- **Purpose:** User is monitoring agent activity with a host app; asked to run agents doing independent work to observe.
- **What ran:** 4 subagents in parallel — Explore (`.claude/` structure survey, read-only), general-purpose (README/framework summary, read-only), developer (created sample utility code), architect (created sample design doc). All completed cleanly.
- **Files created/modified:**
  - `01_Project/src/stringUtils.ts` — slugify/truncate/titleCase pure functions (sample content)
  - `01_Project/tests/stringUtils.test.ts` — 19 Vitest cases for the above
  - `02_solution/url-shortener-design.md` — illustrative one-page design spec
  - State files (TASKS.md, STATUS.md, this file) to satisfy the Stop hook
- **Tests status:** tests written but not run (no package.json / Vitest installed; intentional — files are sample content).
- **Contract changes:** none.
- **Decisions made:** none (sample artifacts only, not committed).
- **Next session should:** treat created files as throwaway sample content. If real work begins, run /analyse then /plan to initialize the project properly. Sample files can be deleted with no impact.

- **Task IDs touched:** none (not real framework work)
